step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the initial conclusion that a low apparent volume of distribution (Vd) suggests the drug is not extensively distributed to tissues. One additional reason is that drugs with low Vd tend to have higher concentrations in the bloodstream due to their affinity for plasma proteins, limiting their ability to diffuse into extravascular spaces. This supports the idea that such drugs remain largely confined to the circulatory system rather than penetrating deeper into organs or tissues.

My answer choice is: D.
